Taking time off to be the best Dad I can be while figuring out what comes next. It's been really valuable to step away from work for a bit and think about what I actually want to do with my career and what problems are worth dedicating my time to solve as a designer.

Led various web projects for the Design and Architecture faculty, producing digital and print materials to support university communications and academic programs. My first paid design job while I was still at university. Made Wordpress websites for different clients, got much needed experience talking to clients directly and figuring out what they actually needed versus what they said they wanted. It was a great way to learn both the design and technical sides of web development, which ended up being really useful throughout my career.

Wellington-based design and print company providing various digital and print services including web design and WordPress development.

Joined Facebook when they were just about to hit 1 billion users and everything was moving incredibly fast. Designed features and new products for Messenger, Photos, and Feed - basically touching the core sharing experiences that billions of people use every day. Worked with PMs, engineers, and other designers to ship features that taught me how to design for massive scale and global audiences.

Social media platform connecting billions of people worldwide, now part of Meta. Known for innovative design and user experience across multiple products including Facebook, Messenger, and Photos.

I worked with the team at Origami during its major pivot from a mobile shopping app to payment technology for stores. I collaborated directly with the founders, longside two other talented designers, to shape what we should build next. I led design and also contributed development work in Objective-C and Swift for a new Bluetooth payment method across iPhone, iPad, and Android devices. Spent time prototyping ideas and presenting to stakeholders to get buy-in for new features, a first on iOS for Japan at the time.

Partnered with the Chief Marketing Officer and Chief Product Officer to refresh Starboard's website, improving content discovery and lead generation while transitioning off legacy technology. Redesigned and built more than 20 new pages on Webflow, including migration of all content, integrations, and clear maintenance documentation for in-house teams. Did end-to-end design and development work for their maritime intelligence platform. The work had real impact at the forefront of their business - helping them better communicate their value to enterprise customers in the security industry.

My first real design job was as an intern at RESN, one of the best digital agencies in New Zealand. I did everything you'd expect from an intern: design, front-end development on projects for big clients like Google and FedEx, and even sweeping the floors. I learned a ton about balancing creative ideas with actually building things that work. The team at RESN was incredible and taught me what great design and technical collaboration look like.

Creative digital agency specializing in immersive web experiences and interactive projects for major clients including Google, FedEx, and EDF.

Joined Rainforest QA right after they went through a major reorganization and pivoted from crowdsourced testing to automated QA tools. Ran design for their developer platform that engineering teams at big tech companies use for testing. The product was pretty complex - had to make sophisticated testing tools actually usable for developers. Worked closely with engineering and product to redesign the core experience while the whole company was figuring out this new direction. Everything was remote which made it interesting during such a big transition.

Automated testing platform that helps QA teams scale their testing efforts through no-code test automation.

Collaborated with a product team of seven to design, build and ship Xero Me - a new mobile app focused on improving employer-employee communication around payroll and time-off. Led the interface design for the iOS app from initial concept through launch. Also got my hands dirty developing iOS components in Swift and facilitated user testing sessions to gather insights and validate our design decisions.

Cloud-based accounting software platform providing business financial management solutions for small and medium-sized businesses.
